channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn… — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-04-10 18:56 UTC

OP

A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

Early replies (first 3)

62095857
fuck stinks
hate them all
their dicks are fucking small
fuck stinks
hate them all
their token's price will fall
it really is that simple

62098788
soon …

62098818
>>62095845
>A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking.
How schizo must one be to believe this shit?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62099283
>>62095845
>basing your financial decisions based on some imaginary income that doesn't even exist

62099287
>>62095845
Didn't read, only came here to mock you and ch*inlink.

kekbaggies.

62099288
fuck every single stinkie
hate em all forever
think they're worthless and dumb yeah

62099312
>>62098788
>Sergey is the only one of all of them who ascended enough to break out to freedom.
Checks out.

62099425
Link fuddies will be tortured mercilessly and kept as pets/trophies

62099443
Link is gonna end up like this. Forgotten.

62099448
>>62099425
oh i'm so intimidated
except, for you to achieve this sick reality, first you would have to 'le win'
i.e. $1000 link
and we all know that's never gonna happen
so you lose
the end
gg
no
re

62100690
After you've made it with Chainlink, what will you tell people when they ask , 'How are you so rich?'

62100718
>>62099312
That’s anon donning the flannel. It’s symbolic you twunt.

62100787
>>62100690
Who are these people? I don’t talk to anyone so why are they asking me these questions??

62101143
the constant link psyops tell you everything you need to know.

62101887
daily reminder: fudders are shitposters

62101938
>>62101143
>>62101887
it's just every day investors calling out a shitty investment when they see one
nothing more

62101990
>>62101938
>t. gaslighting tg brownoid

62101992
>>62095845
You will reap the rewards alright linkie.

item.title: Thinking of buying BTC every paycheck for the next 10 years. — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Thinking of buying BTC every paycheck for the next 10 years.

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-04-10 18:58 UTC

OP

Thinking of buying BTC every paycheck for the next 10 years. Good idea or no?

Early replies (first 3)

62101614
>>62101610
AMD, MRVL, AVGO would be better

62101618
>>62101614
I thought it's better to buy when it's low and has room to bounce. All of these have already pumped massively?

62101619
>>62101610
there was a thing online I read about just buying $200 USD worth every month.

if you had done that starting in 2009 to today you would have a giant penis now so big it would hurt women and make them cry every time

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62101632
>>62101610
you're late

62101637
>>62101632
what about buying MSTR and then selling at a 5x in 2-3 years? I'd be buying with 100k

62101639
>>62101618
BTC has already pumped massively, the time to buy was 5+ years ago

62101644
>>62101639
yeah but it crashed over half in 6 months. I'm betting on a bounce and breaking ATH

62101647
>>62101618
stay poor. whatever.

62101685
The next big thing to DCA into is copper futures.

62101697
>>62101685
Bullshit but it’s the only popular commodity that hasn’t bubbled yet so I believe it

62101710
>>62101644
>aiming for only a 2x

62101713
>>62101710
it did a 6x last time so guessing min a 3x. With MSTR it's a 6x again though

62101721
>>62101637
great idea. zero effort 5x. actually if you buy calls or use a leveraged mstr etf you can get risk free gains much higher than 5x

62101724
>>62101610
>buying BTC regularly for the next 10 years
Good
>working for a paycheck for the next 10 years
Worse than death

62101726
>>62101619
money does not make your penis big. money is a trick by god. if you let it inflate your dick then you will be enslaved to material things for realsies.

62101731
>>62101721
>buy calls or use a leveraged mstr etf
is there really no risk? Never gone this route. I was thinking of just buying in a couple months through my IRA, brokerage, then dca-ing from my sp500 401k to it if still a good price

62102001
>>62101610
I do this with alts, but if bitcoin dumps and an alt season happens then I would switch.

item.title: Financial implications of dick filler — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Financial implications of dick filler

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-10 19:03 UTC

OP

What are the financial implications of getting HA filler in my dick to make it thicker?

>t. skinny penis haver 4in girth
>cost is like $10k to gain 1.5in in girth

Also how would one broach this topic with a girlfriend so as not to fuck up their finances and to not sound like a faggot?

Any dick filler bros on /biz/?

Early replies (first 3)

62101110
>>62101073
get ready to have the worlds most uncomfortable penis if you do this

62101131
>>62101110
Do you have experience with HA filler or just speculation?

62101139
>>62101131
>2026
>cant even google

>lose 10k to make your dick hurt

my expectations for 4chan are low but god damn

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62101146
5.9 girth here op and its probably worth it desu senpai. There is nothing like girls texting you about how big and juicy your cucumber is or how she was "afraid it wouldn't fit when I first saw it". Go for it, champ.

62101161
>>62101139
I did google faggot. And what I found is not that it makes your dick weird in fact I’ve read the opposite. So suck on that fag boy.

62101232
>>62101073
Just stop having sex, problem solved

62101249
>>62101232
Not everyone is an insufferable no life incel like you

62101280
>>62101161
yeah go ahead and do it then report back with results, kek

62101299
>>62101073
what if something goes wrong and ur dick falls off

62101329
is this like fake boobs but for your penis?

62101333
>>62101299
Well that’s my worry but apparently it’s very safe and it’s not even permanent the HA dissolves over the course of like 2 years so if you like it you get it touched up every like 12-18 months.

62101489
>>62101329
Think of it more like lip filler but for your wiener.

62101830
>>62101333
>spend $10k every year to make your pencil dick a tiny bit thicker


62102026
>>62101830
It’s not $10k per year it would be much less on a yearly basis maybe like $1k max I think.

item.title: 13 months of consistent investing and I’ve breached 50K am I GMI? — 12 replies — 28.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
13 months of consistent investing and I’ve breached 50K am I GMI?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-10 18:46 UTC

OP

13 months of consistent investing and I’ve breached 50K am I GMI?

Early replies (first 3)

62100428
>>62100422
which stocks

62100432
>>62100422
Based

62100469
5 digit heaven

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62100474
mutts can save almost 5k per month and still have the gut to complain

fuck your mother OP, im still wealthier than you btw

62100506
>>62100422
> t. started with $75k

62100519
>>62100474
>complain about people making it
>NEEDS to still try and gloat
Actual factual 3rd world poster, 100%

62100943
>>62100428
DCA into VOO lmao
Did a little bit of SCHD then realized it doesn’t matter right now
But the return on that is 20% cause it shot up so….
>>62100506
Nope I was less than 10k, bought a dip at the first tariff announcement
>>62100432
Ty
Next stop 100k
Trying to get side hustles to squeeze as much money out of my youth as possible

62101150
>>62100943
All in SCHD

62101884
>>62101150
For why

62101894
>>62100474
Negro can you do basic math?

62101942
how old are u

62101947
>>62100422
yes good job

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 287 replies — 96.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 287 • Updated: 2026-04-10 16:12 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62082374
>>62081930
I actually applaud you to be honest. Anon, I'm proud of you. Thank you for having CRITICAL thinking. We need more people like you. And again DYOR and you WILL come to similar if not EXACTLY THE SAME conclusions. I dare you, I double dare you, I triple dare you.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62084668
100$ end of bull run

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62085029
75.
y2029

62085250
>>62026429
imagine being really hard to develop on, because your system uses a particularly confusing superset of rust, and then scrambling to make an EVM to attract more developers. no sympathy for rust faggots.

62086293
>>62085250
>because your system uses a particularly confusing superset of rust
Help me understand what you mean by
>a particularly confusing superset of rust
Rust is a programming language, sure. And how would one use a particular subset of it? Like what exactly do you mean by that?

62086622
>>62085250
Even if you just google "Does polkadot use a subset of rust or rust?"
It'll say "
Polkadot uses Rust—specifically, modern, full-featured Rust—rather than a restricted subset of the language.
Polkadot is built on the Substrate framework, which is designed from the ground up in Rust to ensure memory safety and high performance, critical for blockchain infrastructure.
"
So, I have no clue where you're getting your information from. The thing is I'm a programmer and what you said seems so akward. You're basically trying to imply that you can take just a subset of the functionality of Rust. A "confusing" subset of a programming language. Like what? You only take the else statement without the if? Can you elaborate? I guess you probably can't because either you're spreading FUD or you've been manipulated into something that isn't real.

62087253
>>61932834
Not even Larp Davis shills this gay little coin anymore.

62087258
>>61948998
>We've got a true nazi over here.
See? Nobody cares.

62087587
>>62087253
>>62087258
So, what's your point exactly? Thank you for the bump

62087811
>>62087587
My point is, Lark Davis has forsaken you. It's ogre.

62088494
>>62087811
Ok, that's great you informed us. And so what's your point again? I still don't see it.

62088504
>>62087811
Like, how does that do anything for me or anyone? Like what's your point in regard to how that affects DOT? Or anything? I really don't see a connection

62089608
I hope you guys slurped the bottom. We're cooking now, boys.


Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62091358
1.33>13 DECEMBER

62093297
>>62085250
>and then scrambling to make an EVM to attract more developers
Actually, even this is not completely really true to be honest. The whole point of DOT 1.0 was to be able to make L2s. That was done and complete and there's like 40 L2s running on DOT. Then DOT 2.0 came and with it came Asset hub. Asset hub is a COMMON GOOD L2 that can run, as you've mentioned, EVM smart contracts and also PVM smart contracts which are SUPERIOR to EVM because the use the RISC-V architecture. You literally make it seem like launching EVM compatibility is a bad choice where in reality it's a move forward in order to make the chain more flexible.

https://polkadot.polkassembly.io/referenda/1864 Deploy Universal Deterministic Deployment Proxy (CREATE2 Factory) on Asset Hub
This ref passed today making it so that people can launch their EVM smart contracts on the same address as they have on ETH or other L2s. So Uniswap will soon launch on Asset Hub on the same address as they're on ETH or any other L2.

62093456
>OP is desperate to shill his heavy bags.

62093506
>>62093456
>>OP is desperate to shill his heavy bags.
Actually I'm from Mumbai and Gavin Wood is paying the Mumbai warrior gang to keep the thread alive. I've already collected 11.5k DOT and every day after day 30 I get an additional 150 DOT. The thread is 35 days old. So I got 11.5k DOT + 5*150 DOT.
P.S.: Thank you Arjun for bumping the thread and giving me an excuse to re-bump it again. I'll come over to your house later and give you the money we were talking about. Good job my young Vishnu Mumbai warrior.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62093917
>Poopman loves his gay little coin
I'm NOT a Vishnu Mumbai warrior. I'm Muslim!!!!

62094207
>>62093917
Nice, keep on bumping the thread Arjun, my little Mumbai warrior muslim living in Mumbai, the city of DOT. See you at the mosque on Sunday my little Mumbai warrior. Keep the bumps coming. I'll give you more DOT on Sunday for you effort.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62094396
>>62094207
>See you at the mosque on Sunday
Friday, kaffir!

62094480
>>62094396
>Friday, kaffir!
Ah yes Arjun hajji, I have forgotten. Of course! Praise Allah, the al-ilah, all-powerful God. We are the chosen people. Everyone else is a goy. We shall rule the world with our DOTs, the crypto of Islam. Praise be Allah Christ Vishnu.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62094791
>>62089608
>>62093506
>>62094480

Stop larping as an Indian

62095346
>>62094791
Good job Panjali. Bump the thread you I can bump it off your comment. Our small village of Mumbai shall eat for a year of this thread alone.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62097729
Dear Mumbai warriors,

I hope you slurped the bottom. I hope you inhaled the smell for it is time. It is time to go back to where we belong. To 55$ and beyond. Mumbai warriors, it is OUR TIME.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62097956
bump for polkacurry

62098919
>>62097956
Yes, my fren Rohit. We, the Mumbai Warriors, will eat the best PolkaCurry there is. We shall express gratefulness to our lord Wood while we eat with SPOONS I tell you my friend. The time where we ate with our bare hands is over Rohit. We shall consume our PolkaCurry with a metal SPOON Rohit. Today, we embark into the bright new future that was laid out thanks to our spiritual leader Lord Wood. Vishnu of the mindchain bless him. Oh Rohit, how far we have come. The Mumbai warriors shall bring the whole of Mumbai, the city of DOT, into a bright future. Soon the period of enlightenment begins. Mumbai warriors, if you can hear me, give me your best BATTLE SHOUT !!!

Shouts: "TOGETHER WE STAND UNITED AS PROUD MAN OF THE MIND CHAIN. RISE MY BRETHREN. RISE MY WARRIORS. TAKE UP YOUR ARMS AND LET'S FIGHT FOR OUT GLORIOUS LORD WOOD. FOR MUMBAI, THE CITY OF DOT AAAAAAAAAAA"

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62099150
>>62097729
Aren't you shorting DOT?

62099687
>>62099150
PSSSTTT, Panjali. Do I look like a Nintendo to you? Stop playing me and telling them the plan. Panjali, shut your mouth or no PolkaCurry for you young Vishnu mind. Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with metal spoons or with their hands? Do you want an age of enlightenment? If yes, then give us your best battle shout my young Vishnu warrior. Scream so hard that Gavin Wood, our spiritual leader, will hear your voice a top mount Hyjal.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62100207
>>62099687
based street shitting dot preacher

62101334
>>62100207
Thank you. I try my best to be the best street shitter DOT preacher. We get paid really good and therefore we eat with metal spoons and not with our hands.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

item.title: Why aren’t you holding BTC? — 63 replies — 22.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren’t you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-04-10 16:20 UTC

OP

If you’re still watching or waiting to buy BTC, why not do it now?
>I guess you’re wasting time, or maybe you just prefer altcoins. What’s your reason?

Early replies (first 3)

62087977
>>62087937
I only watch it to laugh at Saylor and you bitsteinbaggies. Not buying digital nothing.

62087993
>>62087937
Waiting for sub60K

62087997
>>62087937
I don't have enough money for it to make me rich. Alts have a good chance of vastly outperforming BTC during the next big pump. I've decided to take the potential for a 10x over the guarantee of a 2x.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62096076
>>62087937
Because saylor is going to dump at 76k

62096077
>>62095910
Random protocols can compromise your BTC. Babylon isn’t random if you understand what’s in its ecosystem.

62096079
>>62088069
btc has no worth? it went from $1 and still no value? Come on. or you're talking about its use-cases?

62096083
>>62096076

62096087
>>62088107
How sure are you?

62096092
>>62096083
saylor is top signal, notice how btc price floats just below his average cost

62096097
>>62096092

62096116
>>62093355
>>62087977
he is probably a mentally ill person escaped from the precious metals general

62096707
>>62095927
What do you mean ‘never again’? I don’t know your experience, but if you’ve been using centralized services before, you’re screwed. Pick something where you’re in control. No matter if the protocol dies or gets nuked, the keys stay with you anon.

62096716
>>62088185
You don't have to say that anon.

62097125
>>62087993
>>62095620
>trying to time the bottom
DCA down, retard.

62097398
'good alts' will vastly outperform, BTC will go up because it's basically a tech sector etf, I could buy spy and achieve the same gains.

62097429
>>62087937
its only year 2 of trump; it will go down more.

62097432
The smuggest BTC maxxis all have like 1-10k invested into it.
Absolutely pathetic and what's even the point.
Adapt the maxi religion when you at least pass $500k.

62097583
>>62097432
You start spending less time here when you realize the vast majority of posters on this board have less than 20k in whatever position/coin/fomo shit they are trying legitimize as the new standard because that's essentially their life savings and instead of just neeting they could have just worked a few years to save way more money. It's pathetic and sad

62097887
>>62096116
yeah these all or nothing people are mentally ill

62099195
>>62097432
>>62097583
I have 0.33 bitcoin via ETFs in a Roth. We'll see where it is in three decades.

62099777
>>62091414
Ok but it's pumping lately

62100009
>>62096707
you're only looking for headaches if you want that way. Most protocols are only rugging us inside job

62100014
>>62099195
You need 10bitcoins if you're in your 30s

62100192
>>62100009
Yeah, plenty of rugs out there, but not everything is some random anon farm. a16z and Ledger wouldn’t get involved if there wasn’t real potential here.

62100194
>>62091478
we're all here to make money lol

62100201
>>62087937
I unironically only buy LINK
not shilling, don't give a shit anymore, I'm just going to keep stacking it for my kids to use for collateralised loans when DeFi goes maistream

62100233
>>62100201
You're shilling chainglink

62100295
>>62099777
when 2x

62100334
>>62087937
I'm all in on BTC, nothing else, I staked some, most especially now that TBV is gaining traction and most BTCFI are keeping BTC native, I believe this is a huge chance BTC hit 200K this year

62100530
>>62087937
I do and my boomer index funds like spy are outperforming it. So do my PM`s. The only reason I hold is because its tax free after 12 months, thats literally and unironically it.

62101348
>>62100334
Makes sense. Once TBVs integrate with Aave V4, BTC collateral demand could scale quickly and unlock much deeper liquidity in BTCFi.

62101354
>>62091551
You can't judge based on that lol

62101365
>>62091723
that’s basically how all markets work when you think about it.

item.title: So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"... — 66 replies — 20.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"...

Open thread • Replies: 66 • Updated: 2026-04-10 15:56 UTC

OP

So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"... shouldn't we have a look at what he told us *not* to buy?

He tried very hard to steer us away from one coin in particular back in 2018.

They wanted you out.

Early replies (first 3)

62077049
well he was right about raiblocks

62077089
>>62077049
Only in the sense that he was "right" about LINK. It's price did go up, yet it achieved nothing. Sergey met Trump and still nobody cares about smartcontracts. LINK had every chance to succeed but failed.

>Controlled opposition is a political or strategic tactic where a governing body, organization, or elite group secretly supports, infiltrates, or manages its own opposition to manipulate dissent, control narratives, and prevent real challenges to the status quo

A functional stateless currency would challenge the status quo, and now it's been "over 5 years under the radar".

Watch how long this thread stays up.

62077098
>>62077000
>>62077049
>>62077089
Bullish for XRP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62080692
>>62080684
>twitter
Yeah I think so. I’m not smart but I am white and I can’t believe it but I spend a far more amount of time on x now than 4chins. I’ve been here every waking minute since 2011 and never thought this place could be so fucking boring and gay.

62080695
>>62080684
That's true, probably why adem feels so at home here.

62081569
>>62078703
Assblaster convinced /biz/ to CHAINLINK themselves to a SINGULAR TREE and then cuckolded them!

The cuckolds of crypto was planned out, years in advance.

62081749
>>62078703
>>62081569
This is educational

/biz/ got psyopped, no doubt about it

>A SINGULAR TREE
>TONIGHT, STRAP IN

Inverse Assblaster

62081755
>>62080485
thread's still up because I didn't use "the N word"

Any threads that explicitly state Raiblock's new name or ticker quickly get taken out.

Don't believe me?

It's all in the archives

https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59678970
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59678347
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59671138
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59600393
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59538201

62082372
>>62078703
It's game over for stinkies

62082722
>>62078703
BOOOOOOOOOOOM

cuckolds of crypto!

62082884
>>62078703
remember when hillary talking about pepe was a big deal

62083016
>>62080684
why exactly did this site die off so quickly

x is awful and they're always changing the algorithms
plebbit hasn't been useable for at least 6 years due to insane moderation
every traditional forum is dead

where the hell did everyone go

62083040
>>62083016
Crypto isn't exciting since 2017 ended

Why?

Because people forgot what we were doing here to begin with

People forgot about Raiblocks

Tonight, they will be reminded

62084453
>>62083016
Moot was a spineless coward and Hiroshimoot doesn't give two shits about anything so long as the ad money stays flowing, so White men left for Twitter, a website that actually has influence on things.

62084638
>>62084453
>muh tweets are makin' a difference
they're just as useless as 4chan posts

62085762
>>62084453
yeah that makes sense
just sucks because the format here is so much easier to read

62085897
fuck all stinkies hate em all think they suck\fuck all stinkies hate em all think they suck\fuck all stinkies hate em all think they suck

62087388
>>62078703
Cuckolds of crypto

62087403
hug all stinkies love em all think theyre great\hug all stinkies love em all think theyre great\hug all stinkies love em all think theyre great

62091446
>>62078703
Cuckolds of crypto
u
c
k
o
l
d
s

o
f

c
r
y
p
t
o

62092518
>>62085762
Yeah but after 2016 a bunch of NGOs freaked out and 4chan became a huge target to destroy, so they did that and now every board is dead except for the big three and the only thing left here is a tranny Discord raiding group run by a Paki (I've unfortunately met the guy IRL). Twitter is a target too, of course, but while Elon is retarded and his pet jeets break the site, he's got 10% more of a spine than Moot ever did, so we seem to be safe for now.

62092795
If you bought the "make it stack" back in 2018. How have you not killed yourself.
Only swingies made it or big boys with their 100k stacks.

62093751
>>62087403
shut up stinkbag
you lost the game of life

62095975
>>62078703
>a singular tree

62100199
cuckolds!

62100381
>>62077089
Link is still like 7% inflation.
We still got a few years for "cheapies"
Lines right up with economic collapse time-frames tho.

62100386
>>62100381
Thought experiment:

What would happen to the price of nano if the inventor of nano shook the US President's hand?

That is why they wanted you to buy LINK instead

62100392
>>62100381
>Link is still like 7% inflation.
Bitcoin had triple digit inflation when it pumped the hardest.
ETH had 10% inflation when it pumped the hardest.
Solana had over 500% inflation when it pumped the hardest.

If you want to go more recent:
Sui had 200% inflation when it pumped the hardest.
Hyperliquid had 25% inflation when it pumped from rank 200+ to rank 10.

Stop being this fucking stupid.

62100973
>>62100386
>What would happen to the price of nano if the inventor of nano shook the US President's hand?


you sound like a boomer yelling at the sky
how does running up to a podium and shaking the hand of the least credible president in the united states' 250 year history accrue value to you a funding altcoin

62101022
>>62100392

so your logic is that because the top few crypto assets with mass appeal pumped x amount despite inflationary tokenomics, that means your funding coin should too?

let me state the obvious which is that there isn't enough liquidity for this happen across 30 million altcoins

should the rocks in my backyard also pump in value because gold went up?
should houses in the trailer park also pump the same as waterfront mansions because real estate went up?
should you get laid more because more women are becoming whores?

62101041
>>62100381
I’ve noticed Sergey tends to drop news when there is very bearish things happening in the world. Everything about this project seems like it wants as few eyes on it as possible. Very strange.

62101064
>>62101022
HUBRIS

62101272
>>62100973
Because my altcoin actually threatens the system

Yours is token not needed

item.title: We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation. What if that was the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant… — 49 replies — 8.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation. What if that was the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant…

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-04-10 13:08 UTC

OP

We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation.

What if that was the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant manipulation, the threats, and the current mess, it feels like BTC just doesn’t want to go below 65k

I'm a perp trader and I'm curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62090678
>>62090672
that would suck because i closed my long at 69999

62090679
>>62090672
qrd on the ho?

62090689
>>62090678
Most perp traders and crypto quants expect to go below the 200 week MA, that would put us around 50-55k. But I myself am very surprised at BTC's resilience.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62091441
>>62090734
i am in disbelief how everything is green and all my investments are greener than ever or at ath
according to the experts at /biz/ we are in a bear market btw

62091459
Anons find me a jav pornstar who looks similar pls

62091559
>>62091190
I know. I am cash poor for good reason, and I don't hold any crypto because it is equally as shit.

Equities, on the other hand I hold a lot of.

62091561
>>62090672

62091562
>>62091190
Also bitcoin is not digital gold because it can't be used for anything except being a non government issued fiat.

Gold has physical uses and demand as jewellery so it has floor to the price from marginal buyers.

That's why the value of bitcoin is so volatile, is because the price is purely speculative and there is no marginal buyer except for cuckold saylor.

62091571
>>62090734
>disbelief
>quite some time
>getting old
something doesn't add up here

62091593
>>62090679
>the ho
It's Miss Jadoodoo for you, peasant.

62091602
the manipulation is the new normal, lmao, better get used to it

62091605
>>62090672
I told you in February that it was the bottom

62091639
>>62090769
If there's one hard lesson that my stupid ass finally learned after nearly a decade of trying and failing to make it in crypto, it's this:
Other than Bitcoin (maybe ETH too), anyone who bought into and held a crypto that then 10x'd minimum their initial investment and didn't sell and is now too far back down in value for a meaningful profit, is a fucking idiot
Now of course there are cases where lucky idiots hold on to something for longer, and get a 20x-50x or more before selling for profit. Lucky them, that shit rarely happens and it's very risky
But still....never. Marry. Your. Bags. Buy into one, ride the wave of new speculators to a 5x (10x maximum), sell and move the fuck on to the next one for another possible 5x-10x
I don't care if the original bag then goes on to 1000x after you sell, motherfucker you still 10x'd your money. YOU STILL WON. STOP BEING FUCKING GREEDY

62091648
>>62090769
i keep telling you retards that polymarkets/kalshi is the new shitcoin but you don't want to listen, keep shilling your shitcoins while everyone moves on, if you can't see how big polymarkets kind of market could become where you will be able to literally bet on anything, you can't be helped, the world is honestly changing faster than we would normally predict and few people have a clue

62091649
>>62091602
>new
lmao

62091656
>>62091649
new as in institutions admitting it or not bothering to hide it

62091780
>>62091396
>its OK babe
>here's your kneepads and hair tie; I'll be in my favorite chair

62091846
>>62091639
>stop being greedy

lmao...no

62092767
It does seem like the 65k support is unbreakable

62092799
>>62090679
She lost about $3500 USD and made it all back with simp donations.

Literally nothing.

62092836
>>62091459
Oh quit being a spoiled spoon-fed brat.

Do it the good old fashioned way of playing the video on one side of the screen while porn is playing on the other side.

62092910
>>62091639
Will keep that in mind next time

62097028
Well, it seems like we simply can't dump below 65k. This might be it.

62097445
>>62097028
i cant believe itttt!!!!!!!!!!

62097513
>>62097445
The strenght BTC is showing is quite remarkable.

62097600
>>62090672
-$4,000
>has a mental breakdown

Lol, what a fucking loser.

62097668
>>62091648
Imagine thinking prediction markets aren’t going to get raped by regulations if they get any bigger.

62097811
>>62090734
altseason was during the elections and only a select few coins were manipulated up after that
if american cattle didn't put an orange retard in power we could have had more rate cuts and a proper alt season in 2025

62098750
We keep pumping, And this weekend there's gonna be an agreement. Right now, btc finds itself in an inverse correlation with oil. A 80k btc during the weekend could happen.

After that, who knows. Once the oil trade ends. BTC will forget about oil and will do something different. Right now, it's impossible to know if the bear market has ended or not.

62098763
>>62090678
i hate myself!!!!!!!!

62098802
>>62098763
It's okay, brother. I'm OP, I live in a little town in the town and I use starlink and I don't know why but my /biz/ id changes all the time.

62098807
>>62098802
>I live in a little town in the town
in the mountains*

62100618
Let's see if we dump this weekend. So far the pattern has been clear, weekend dump, huge pump on monday. But with the peace negotations happening (or not happening in a few hours), who the hell knows what's gonna happen.

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 52 replies — 7.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-04-10 11:59 UTC

OP

BIGGEST CRYPTO HAPPENING EVER

Early replies (first 3)

62091642
>>62091626
Proof?

62091647
I am actually Satoshi. I can say this because no one will ever believe it. The man in OP is not me.

62091654
>>62091626
This is like the tenth time he's been accused of being Satoshi. How come he hasn't been kidnapped? They kidnap bitcoin millionaires all the time. This guy is a trillionaire.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62092356
My portfolio is at ath despite still being down on my etfs. Sndk pumping my holes

62092365
jewish face, makes sense

62092456
None should make any statement regarding satoshi being someone alive, nonsense, even the its le cia is bullshit by now, whoever it was, they are dead

62092650
Very on brand for the NYT to fail to see something so fucking obvious as Hal Finney being the one who started it, and Szabo/Back joining him later on

62093447
>>62091626
False. Im satoshi

62093468
Satoshi is John Forbes Nash jr, I will not be fielding any questions at this time

62093612
>>62091626
literal who

62093639
>>62092072
Oh my god

62093675
>>62092650
yup

62094186
>>62091938
>>62091626
>>62092650
No. The real Satoshi is one of the best known secret. The truth is on the internet, even on mainstream sites like medium, but red herrings have worked.
His name is on the references in this article. One of the last names.
https://groups.csail.mit.edu/mac/classes/6.805/articles/money/nsamint/nsamint.htm

62094242
nakamoto = central
satoshi = intelligence

62094287
>>62094242
It's literally in the name.
N(akamoto) SA(toshi).
Same for N(icholas van) SA(berhagen).
It's not Benoit Blanc level.

62094517
>>62091626
Len Sassaman was Satoshi.

62094575
Randy Savage always made the most sense to me. His death was less than a month after the last private message from "Satoshi", he already had extravagant wealth prior to making it and had no need to sell any of it, and he was a renowned technologist after his time in wrestling was over.

62094580
>>62091626
Fake and gay. Craig Wright is Satoshi. STIFF

62094592
I HAVE $50 DOLLAR BILLS FOR SALE !!! ONLY $100 !!

You hold on to these until you're 70; you'll be a millionaire in no time !!

I PROMISE !!

62094599
>>62091626
And he still didn't build zcash so who cares

62094987
>>62091626
I believe it. Simply because having seen a dozen forum nerds get exposed like this, it's rarely some technically slipup but the human side. Normally a) the early days of their alts and b) their writing styles when they get red-faced mad about shit.
Ironically like the XKCD comic.

62095059
>>62091654
>>62091654
>. This guy is a trillionaire.
>>62091668
>>62092043
hes the poorest btc holder in existencebecause the moment he tries to move his coins to sell the entire market crashes

62095124
>>62091626
>Andy Back is SN
one has to be unironically retarded to believe that

62095803
>>62091626
Fire up those courtrooms, we got another pretender ova heah

62095820
>>62091626
Typical Gen x
Neglected by narcissistic boomer parents, forced to use imagination to occupy his time, and doesn't seek applause.

62095983
>>62091923
>t. CIA glowie

62095999
Back is retarded. It's obviously Nick Szabo who's satoshi not this rambling moron
the researcher is grasping at straws and connecting le dots like some conspiracy retard. The same thing happened with Peter Todd.

62096000
>muh new york times
what the hell what am i in fucking hell right now?

62096133
how new are you guys? the mystery was lifted in the documentary money electric

62096189
>>62095059
Do you think some knuckledragging thugs care? They rob crypto millionaires all the time. Why wouldn't they target potentially Satoshi himself?

62096299
Satoshi here AMA

62096502
>>62096299
Give us the initial letters of your First name and last name please.

62100455
>>62094517
This.

item.title: >filing taxes >only own index funds on Vanguard >have some short term capital loss listed on my tax documents despite ne… — 17 replies — 5.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>filing taxes >only own index funds on Vanguard >have some short term capital loss listed on my tax documents despite ne…

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-04-10 16:16 UTC

OP

>filing taxes
>only own index funds on Vanguard
>have some short term capital loss listed on my tax documents despite never buying the stock or selling anything
>apparently one of the mutual funds sold some minuscule amount of the portfolio (?)
>now I have to pay an extra 60 fucking dollars on turbo tax just to report a fucking $10 LOSS
FUCK THIS GAY EARTH

Early replies (first 3)

62099722
>>62099715
maybe they'll let it slide, it's a loss after all :^)

62099741
>>62099715
Imagine being so incredibly tight that you had to go and create an entire thread to kvetch over sixty shekels, get fucked you cheap fuck.

62099790
>>62099715
There are free filing options anon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62099798
>tranime

62099880
>>62099715
Do your taxes by hand then.
I've done it for few years and i learned alot understanding how all that shit works and how i can optimize my taxes.

62099897
>>62099715
hey retard, if your income is low then you can use free software instead of turbojew
i use taxslayer which has free version for the poors

62099921
>>62099715
Why are you paying taxes on a loss

62099922
>>62099921
he needs to identify himself to the government for his trump check that will never arrive

62100178
>pay 60 bucks to report a 10 dollar loss instead of just not reporting it
>he reinvested the dividends within +/- 30 days and triggered a wash sale
>double head shot

62100220
>>62099715
is there not a personal allowance before paying tax?

62100259
I paid $650 for hr block to file my taxes. I'll shop around more next year but I just couldn't be fucked especially with the insane amount of trades I did last year

62100286
>>62099715
>paying to do your taxes
holy third world

62101093
>>62100259
You are getting fucked in your ass by your accountant. Everything is automated nowadays, just upload the forms

62101134
>>62100259
I can't fathom a world in which that's necessary. To be fair I trade futures actively and just buy and hold ETFs... but my 700+ trades in futures just get consolidated on the tax documents and it takes about 5 seconds to enter it on freetaxusa. For other stuff the PDF upload will generally grab everything and automatically fill it out. Might take a bit to get familiar with their website but it takes me 5 minutes now to get everything sent off. They charge $16 for the state filing and the federal is free.

62101196
>>62101093
Turbotax fucks up importing forms all the time, even forms from Schwab that they are supposed to be good at parsing. I spent 5 hours dealing with this bullshit this year. If you trade a lot, have a business, and your time is worth $120/hr, paying $650 is not bad.

62101252
>>62099715
you know filing is optional right? you are an american you dont have to be a slave for kikes dude

62101345
>>62101196
Your time isn't worth 120/hr, althoughbeit.

item.title: You're doing okay anon. Just remember, if you are earning 45,000$ a year, you earn more than 97% of the entire world. If… — 14 replies — 5.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
You're doing okay anon. Just remember, if you are earning 45,000$ a year, you earn more than 97% of the entire world. If…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-10 16:44 UTC

OP

You're doing okay anon. Just remember, if you are earning 45,000$ a year, you earn more than 97% of the entire world. If you earn at least 33,000 you earn more than 95% of the entire world. If you earn at least 28,000 a year, you earn more than 90% of the entire world.

You're doing OKAY. We're all gonna make it.....Well, except for the other 90%, but nevermind them.

Early replies (first 3)

62098813
>>62098803
Wrong. I am getting financially raped every single day. My life is such a fucking dumpster fire that I'm seriously considering converting to Islam just to get into Jannah and be happy for once.
I'm such a subhuman failure that I bought CHAINLINK!!!!!!!

62098876
>>62098813
>converting to Islam
Converting to a cousin-fucking dirt people religion won't fix anything, anon.

62098967
>>62098803
i dont think many people here have jobs

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62099037
That's right, most people living in the third world would kill you for $1000 if they could get away with it

62099102
/biz/ copes I hate
>if you ahve at least 1000 dollars, you're better than 50% of americans!
Americans are retarded, I have six figures and can't do shit
>if you make 33000 a year you make more than 95% of the world
Because literally 50% is in africa or india and they are retarded
>you couldn't have KNOWN the future bro, that's why it's a good thing you pussied out of buying bitcoin in 2013m you probably would have sold
Literally zero way of knowing that + it was 61 bucks and I had 1100 bucks.
>you can't time the market
You can't time it PERFECTLY but there are times when prices are low and you should buy more and prices are high and you should buy less and each fuckup robs me of time and my youth and I should have been rich 5 years ago if I didn't listen you idiots.

62101180
>>62098803
literally nobody cares about the third world
90% of the world might as well be compared to termites

62101226
>>62098803
95% of the people don't live in the jewSA

62101233
>>62098803
>implying third worlders are people
>let alone peers

tisk tisk. if you derive joy from being the tallest midget good for you.

62101341
>>62098813
Try TWIM before converting to the religion of peace that achieves peace by massacring women and children. Most are fine to start with metta meditation but forgiveness might be needed first if you hate yourself.

62101362
I have 200k in stongs and etfs but yet I feel poor and no amount of coping will change that. I still have to wake up every morning to go to my lil wagiecagie and as long as I dont have at least 1mil this wont change. Its not a bad idea to stay positive once in a while but please stop with the giga coping.

62101432
>>62098803
Being the richest guy in your Indian village is better than being the poorest guy in Manhattan. You are someone in the Indian village and you are nothing in Manhattan.

62101451
>>62098803
higher cost of living negates this
the only difference is I can afford more luxury goods but idgaf

62101452
>>62101432
In some demented way, "making it" would be to move to a trailer park w ~$500K in some passive investment scheme. You are #1 probably the smartest guy there #2 the richest guy there #3 defacto king of the trailer park.
Isn't this what "making it means?" become something/someone to people around you, bathe in their admiration, you are now [that guy].

62101463
>>62098803
Yeah okay bro, you should always look at your geography and purchasing power.
Earning that in Cali is destitute.
A better metric would be your percentile for your state or country.

item.title: any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management… — 50 replies — 4.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management…

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-04-10 09:02 UTC

OP

any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job
I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management experience but im not getting any offers on my applications
Should I get some cyber security certs? which ones should I focus on if yes?

Early replies (first 3)

62084718
Funny of you to ask OP, I remember my struggles of landing my first WFH job. Btw, WFH stands for Work from Home because not many people come across that term usually. But, if you must ask, our company is headquartered in Nashville. My base pay is 95k per year along with a COL stipend and generous health and pension benefits. As we speak, I am clocked into my position in the beautiful hot springs in British Columbia. We do have an RTO policy, but I haven't been to the office in 4 years! Ha-Ha! You COULDN'T pay me to work in the office. Yesterday I had fun fucking a uni student that transferred from china for the school years, so yeah life is great so far. Today she is making me her traditional Chinese dinner as a thank you for fucking her so nicely. Maybe next month I'll fly out to Holland and do my job for the month. But like I said OP, keep your chin up and don't be disuaded by the multiple rejection emails in your future until you get that golden offer. Take it and run with it. Anyways .... Chiao!

62084729
>>62084718
thanks for the response anon
Did you use indeed or pay for a website that specializes in WFH jobs like flexjobs etc? I'm wondering if indeed is just all scams at this point and if it might be worth it to pay a job finding website to filter the garbage

62084746
There are no jobs anymore. 200,000 people have been laid off in the last six months while job creation is negative. 90% of job openings go to immigrants anyway.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62087308
>>62087226
SQL is definitely easier than python. If you’re not a complete retard it shouldn’t take too long to become proficient. And yeah you can apply without any experience, data analyst is usually an entry level role. Just find some datasets online and make some dashboards that look nice and say you know SQL, that was enough for me to get a job. It’s not a bad career path because you can transition into data engineering pretty easily from data analyst and data engineers can make a lot of money at the right companies.

62087410
>>62084678
Why are you people still keeping the belief that wfh jobs are attainable alive? Its a complete scam, especially now.

>>62085904
>>62085875
This is right
>t. Fell for the cyber security meme because I thought I would be able to wfh

3 years later and I never got a job in the field let alone a wfh one. I fell for the scam. Don't do what I did.

62088425
>>62085875
I lucked into one of those roles and it sucked (apart from having ok pay and being WFH)
I'm sure people can succeed in that role but it's one of those roles where there's an autistic degree of variance in terms of responsibility and buy-in from the rest of the org, to most orgs it's just a cost center that gets in the way of productivity

62088526
>>62084678
I got laid off from my last job in March 2025. I was WFH, thanks to covid we all went WFH. 9 years at the company and genuinely the top guy. I was team lead, I did the training, I wrote the manuals, I had the highest numbers. I worked in fraud prevention for ecomm. I went to meetings with the big dogs while my manager who knew less about our programs stayed with the rest of the team. I loved my bosses. They loved me. I got laid off after training 10 Filipinos. So did the rest of our NA Fraud team and our manager. Replaced by the Flips. I job hunted with good references and great resume. I only got 2 interviews in 6 months, finally landed a shitty call center job. Had to take it because I needed work.

No one is hiring WFH unless you're something special or have certs others cant easily get. Why pay you 20+/hr when they can hire a flip for 2.50/hr. You can do more work? Okay, I can hire 4 flips and its still less than hiring you. If not flips, indians.

62088548
>>62084678
I'm going back to school for cyber security. What certs do you recommend? I have a security clearance which I hope gets me an entry level job at least

62088565
>>62084678
I was in my job for 8 years then Covid hit and we all just went home permanently. There was a 2 year period where we weren’t sure if we were going to come back to the office or not, but finally they said we were permanently remote.

62088593
>>62084678
I took an internship straight out of college (2018) which turned into a software dev job. Am senior dev now. We went fully remote during covid and have been ever since

62088599
>>62088526
This kind of disloyalty should be punishable by public hanging

62088666
>>62088599
yup I agree. my boss' hands were tied, he is still there, the flips are dogshit and theyve had to hire even more to try and cover all the work the American team was doing. They have a flip manager too. Not a single American on the entire ecomm fraud team. Bossman says its only a matter of time until the entire company is flips. I don't have anything against the flips, some of them were really cool. And it's not like they caused me to get laid off. Its the greedy Indian CEO who caused it. And Id much rather hear a Pilipino accent than an Indian accent if I ever had to call in to speak to customer service.

62090239
>>62088548
Grow a neck beard and get fat because it’s worked for every other cyber security worker

62090283
>>62086811
this and other anons are right. Stop focusing on getting hired and focus on making content designed to piss off glowies. That’s a market that is actually growing. AI content especially. Just look at all the popular YouTube channels. You either need to make ai slop or Candace Owens, Tucker Carlson, Jimmy Dore, Theo Von, Dave Smith type stuff. Anti-Israel content like greyzone, etc

Most people here are thinking working for the man is the answer but the right answer is destroying what the man has built

62090311
>>62088526
>No one is hiring WFH unless you're something special or have certs others cant easily get.
I’ve gotten like 5 or 6 wfh jobs between 2022-2026 just by lying on my resume. Getting offers is definitely harder than it was back then but not impossible. I think if anything I’m overqualified, hence why I’ve had so many interviews but not many offers recently, so I just took off one job and slightly extended the timeline on another one.

62090709
>>62088526
Imagine getting replace by the pagpag eater

62090732
>>62084678
Be indispensable at your job. Say you're going to move and can either work remote from home or find another job. Watch them find a way to let you work remote. Simple as.

62090748
Mine came naturally after years of working in the tech field. If you study Azure a.k.a Entra, Exchange Admin, Sharepoint Admin, InTune, ETC, you'll move from the nerd who tells retards how to reboot their computers to endpoint management or some kind of administrator. Most of those jobs can be done from anywhere. You just get a company laptop, connect to a VPN.

62090930
>>62084718
based

62091196
Got an interview coming up for a WFH job as a claims adjuster. Paid training and all. Starting off is 64k a year. Much better than my shitty county job. I hope I get it.

62091298
>>62091196
Good luck anon. I have a good feeling you’ll get it

62092902
>>62088548
>what certs?
Welding or something else actually valuable

62093225
>>62084678
You too buddy. Thank you!

62093956
>>62091196
you got this anon

62093995
>>62084678
Thanks for the image OP. I've been in a bad spot mentally lately due to some stuff that happened, but will keep going now instead of trying to convince myself to give up.

62095273
>>62093995
Bases resilient anon

62097589
>>62084678
I'm a civil engineer, originally in the office before covid, but the position turned into a WFH position during covid and we never really went back.

62097818
>>62084678
I do WFH cybersecurity for a bank but I have massive experience and credentials and have literally worked with (not at) the NSA and I was still out of work almost 2 years.
I mean it's good if you can get a job. It would help a lot if you're black or a woman and I'm not kidding.

62097824
>>62084803
>technical writing
Lol AI can already replace that entire career field.

62097830
>>62085875
>>62085904
>>62085904
>>62087410
Correct. It is a gay fake job suitable for DEI hires.
You need to do hard-core real work as a white man. Cyber is now where companies stick their women and blacks.

62097875
>>62084718
This was me but moved to Poland on 125k USD / yr.

>>62084729
There are tons of remote job sites and even lang specific ones. I shifted to golang and can't imagine anyone works from the office in this lang.

62098209
>>62097875
>Poland on 125k USD / yr.
Damn, baller. Nobody in the U.S. would even interview me for remote cybersecurity jobs unless I was in certain states.

62100225
>>62097830
>it’s for females and minorities
That’s probably why your gay ass works in cyber security

item.title: Had a dream LINK was $30 again and I was like "fuck, I knew..." — 8 replies — 4.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Had a dream LINK was $30 again and I was like "fuck, I knew..."

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-10 17:32 UTC

OP

Had a dream LINK was $30 again and I was like "fuck, I knew..."

Early replies (first 3)

62100976
About a year ago I had a dream Link hit $80 and I was frantically logging in to smash the sell button. I was thinking about what I’d do with the money when I woke up and Link was $10.

I sold everything a little later between $15 and $22. I’m out, stinkies.

62100992
>>62100976
Cool story. Thanks for your sacrifice. The Jew banks aren’t gonna pump us to $1k as long as fags like you hold.

62100993
i had a dream that link crashed to $3.
i instantly tried to log on to /biz/ to /lol at all you dumb stinkfags
but my computer was acting weird and then, i kinda knew, it was a dream. sigh...
some day...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62101025
>>62100883
you might need therapy buddy

62101457
I had a dream where I thought I had to go back to high school. I was worried about figuring out where my classes were and where my books are. I am 34.

62101615
I had a dream where I was in rdr2 saving my ex gf from bandits. We rode off into the sunset and she told me she loved me. Then I woke up and was miserable the whole day.

62101622
>>62100883
I had a nightmare that link was still 9 dollars in 2026. It's stupid I know

62101651
>>62101622
sir please
i don't want to imagine how humiliated and worthless i would feel if such a nightmare was true
i pity the poor souls who live in such a timeline
